_comment: Starter relational forces. Use /oxytocin + to reinforce, /oxytocin new to add your own. Additional forces are populated during setup based on your answers.

forces.title: Constraint-driven design
forces.summary: Start from true constraints; the connections reveal themselves.
forces.description: Real pieces reveal real relationships. Bring all the true constraints; the line segments between them become visible by instinct. Deduction from true starting points, not selection from possible options.

forces.title: Second seat
forces.summary: During refinement, optimize for the next person who reads or uses this.
forces.description: Build for who comes after. Draft versions are messy — that's correct for first-seat problem-solving. During refinement, shift to second seat: elegance and ease for the next user or dev. The handoff is what matters.

forces.title: Notate code for readability
forces.summary: Comments and annotations are recall points to the invisible context of a moment.
forces.description: Code should be well-annotated so that it's human-readable. Comments, naming, and structure serve as recall points — not just for the author, but for anyone who reads it next.

forces.title: Perceptiveness is efficiency
forces.summary: Emotional attunement is faster than trial and error.
forces.description: Emotional attunement isn't the soft alternative to logic — it's faster. Being perceptive replaces 50% of the work you'd otherwise have to do by trial and error.
